message.content:
Search in /home/ryan/formal-conjectures/FormalConjectures/ForMathlib/ for any Lean files that deal with:
- Partition of integers/naturals
- Sum-free sets
- Coloring of integers
- Ramsey theory
- Arithmetic progressions
- Additive combinatorics
- Sets where no element divides another (antichains in divisibility)

Also search the broader FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/ directory for files that might share infrastructure with a problem about partitioning integers into classes where no element in a class divides another. Look for keywords: "partition", "divisibility", "antichain", "divides", "Finpartition", "coloring", "sum-free".

Report file paths and brief descriptions of what you find.

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 164
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/164](https://www.erdosproblems.com/164)
    23â†’
    24â†’[Er35] Erdإ‘s, P., *Note on sequences of integers no one of which is divisible by any other*.
    25â†’J. London Math. Soc. (1935).
    26â†’
    27â†’[Er76g] Erdإ‘s, P., *Problems and results on combinatorial number theory* (1976).
    28â†’
    29â†’[Er86] Erdإ‘s, P., *Some problems and results on combinatorial number theory* (1986).
    30â†’
    31â†’[Li23] Lichtman, J.D., *The Erdإ‘s primitive set conjecture*. Forum Math. Pi (2023).

    34â†’open Real
    35â†’
    36â†’namespace Erdos164
    37â†’
    38â†’/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is primitive (an antichain under divisibility)
    39â†’if no element divides a distinct element: for all $a, b \in A$, $a \mid b \implies a = b$. -/
    40â†’def IsPrimitive (A : Set â„•) : Prop :=
    41â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, a âˆ£ b â†’ a = b
    42â†’
    43â†’/--
    44â†’Erdإ‘s Conjecture (Problem 164) [Er76g, Er86]:
    45â†’
    46â†’For any primitive set $A \subseteq \{2, 3, 4, \ldots\}$ of natural numbers,
    47â†’$$\sum_{n \in A} \frac{1}{n \cdot \log n} \leq \sum_{p \text{ prime}} \frac{1}{p \cdot \log p}.$$
    48â†’
    49â†’That is, the sum $\sum \frac{1}{n \log n}$ over any primitive set is maximised when
    50â†’the set is the set of all primes.
    51â†’
    52â†’Proved by Lichtman [Li23].
    53â†’-/
    54â†’@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    55â†’theorem erdos_164 :
    56â†’    âˆ€ A : Set â„•, IsPrimitive A â†’ A âٹ† {n : â„• | 2 â‰¤ n} â†’
    57â†’    âˆ‘' n : A, (1 : â„‌) / (((n : â„•) : â„‌) * Real.log ((n : â„•) : â„‌)) â‰¤
    58â†’    âˆ‘' p : {p : â„• | Nat.Prime p}, (1 : â„‌) / (((p : â„•) : â„‌) * Real.log ((p : â„•) : â„‌)) := by

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 131
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/131](https://www.erdosproblems.com/131)
    23â†’
    24â†’Let $F(N)$ be the maximal size of $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ such that no $a \in A$ divides
    25â†’the sum of any distinct elements of $A \setminus \{a\}$. Estimate $F(N)$.
    26â†’
    27â†’The specific question "is $F(N) > N^{1/2 - o(1)}$?" has been answered NO by
    28â†’Pham and Zakharov, who proved $F(N) \leq N^{1/4 + o(1)}$ via non-averaging sets.
    29â†’The correct growth rate of $F(N)$ remains open.
    30â†’
    31â†’Known bounds: $N^{1/5} \ll F(N) \leq N^{1/4 + o(1)}$.
    32â†’
    33â†’[Er75b] Erdإ‘s, P., *Problems and results on combinatorial number theory III*, 1975.
    34â†’
    35â†’[Er97b] Erdإ‘s, P., *Some of my new and almost new problems and results in combinatorial
    36â†’number theory*, 1997.
    37â†’
    38â†’[ELRSS99] Erdإ‘s, P., Lev, V., Rauzy, G., Sأ،ndor, C., Sأ،rkأ¶zy, A., *Greedy algorithm,
    39â†’arithmetic progressions, subset sums and divisibility*, 1999.
    40â†’
    41â†’[PhZa24] Pham, T., Zakharov, D., *Non-averaging sets and the Erdإ‘s divisibility conjecture*,
    42â†’2024.
    43â†’-/
    44â†’
    45â†’open Filter
    46â†’
    47â†’namespace Erdos131
    48â†’
    49â†’/-- A finite set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *non-dividing* if for every $a \in A$ and every
    50â†’nonempty $S \subseteq A \setminus \{a\}$, the element $a$ does not divide $\sum_{s \in S} s$. -/
    51â†’def IsNonDividing (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    52â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ S : Finset â„•, S âٹ† A.erase a â†’ S.Nonempty â†’ آ¬(a âˆ£ S.sum id)
    53â†’
    54â†’/-- $F(N)$ is the maximal cardinality of a non-dividing subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
    55â†’noncomputable def erdos131F (N : â„•) : â„• :=
    56â†’  sSup {k | âˆƒ A : Finset â„•, A âٹ† Finset.Icc 1 N âˆ§ IsNonDividing A âˆ§ A.card = k}
    57â†’
    58â†’/--
    59â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 131 [Er75b, Er97b]:
    60â†’Let $F(N)$ be the maximal size of a non-dividing subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$.
    61â†’The open problem is to determine the correct polynomial growth rate of $F(N)$.
    62â†’
    63â†’Known bounds:
    64â†’- $F(N) \geq c \cdot N^{1/5}$ (Csaba; Erdإ‘sâ€“Levâ€“Rauzyâ€“Sأ،ndorâ€“Sأ،rkأ¶zy [ELRSS99])
    65â†’- $F(N) \leq N^{1/4 + o(1)}$ (Phamâ€“Zakharov [PhZa24], via non-averaging sets)
    66â†’
    67â†’This conjecture asserts that the Phamâ€“Zakharov upper bound is essentially tight:
    68â†’$F(N) \geq N^{1/4 - \varepsilon}$ for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and all sufficiently large $N$.
    69â†’-/
    70â†’@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    71â†’theorem erdos_131 :
    72â†’    âˆ€ خµ : â„‌, 0 < خµ â†’
    73â†’      âˆ€ل¶  N : â„• in atTop, (erdos131F N : â„‌) â‰¥ (N : â„‌) ^ ((1 : â„‌) / 4 - خµ) := by
    74â†’  sorry
    75â†’
    76â†’end Erdos131

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 1110
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1110](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1110)
    23â†’
    24â†’Let $p > q \geq 2$ be two coprime integers. We call $n$ *representable* if it is the sum of
    25â†’integers of the form $p^k q^l$, none of which divide each other.
    26â†’
    27â†’If $\{p,q\} \neq \{2,3\}$ then what can be said about the density of non-representable numbers?
    28â†’Are there infinitely many coprime non-representable numbers?
    29â†’
    30â†’[ErLe96] Erdإ‘s, P. and Lewin, M., proved that there are finitely many non-representable numbers
    31â†’if and only if $\{p,q\} = \{2,3\}$.
    32â†’
    33â†’[YuCh22] Yu and Chen proved that the set of representable numbers has density zero for
    34â†’many cases, and that there are infinitely many coprime non-representable numbers for
    35â†’most parameter choices. Some cases remain open.
    36â†’-/
    37â†’
    38â†’open Finset Classical
    39â†’
    40â†’namespace Erdos1110
    41â†’
    42â†’/-- A positive integer $m$ is a $(p,q)$-power if $m = p^a \cdot q^b$ for some $a, b \geq 0$. -/
    43â†’def IsPQPower (p q m : â„•) : Prop :=
    44â†’  âˆƒ a b : â„•, m = p ^ a * q ^ b
    45â†’
    46â†’/-- A finite set of natural numbers is an antichain under divisibility:
    47â†’no element divides a distinct element. -/
    48â†’def IsDivisibilityAntichain (S : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    49â†’  âˆ€ x âˆˆ S, âˆ€ y âˆˆ S, x âˆ£ y â†’ x = y
    50â†’
    51â†’/-- A natural number $n$ is $(p,q)$-representable if $n$ equals the sum of a nonempty finite set
    52â†’of numbers of the form $p^a \cdot q^b$, where no element divides another. -/
    53â†’def IsRepresentable (p q n : â„•) : Prop :=
    54â†’  âˆƒ S : Finset â„•, S.Nonempty âˆ§
    55â†’    (âˆ€ m âˆˆ S, IsPQPower p q m) âˆ§
    56â†’    IsDivisibilityAntichain S âˆ§
    57â†’    S.sum id = n
    58â†’
    59â†’/--
    60â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 1110 [ErLe96]:
    61â†’
    62â†’For coprime integers $p > q \geq 2$ with $\{p,q\} \neq \{2,3\}$, there are infinitely many
    63â†’non-representable numbers that are coprime to $p \cdot q$.
    64â†’
    65â†’Since $p > q \geq 2$ and $p, q$ are coprime, the only excluded case is $p = 3, q = 2$.
    66â†’-/
    67â†’@[category research open, AMS 11]
    68â†’theorem erdos_1110 : answer(sorry) â†”
    69â†’    âˆ€ p q : â„•, 2 â‰¤ q â†’ q < p â†’ Nat.Coprime p q â†’
    70â†’      آ¬(p = 3 âˆ§ q = 2) â†’
    71â†’      Set.Infinite {n : â„• | آ¬IsRepresentable p q n âˆ§ Nat.Coprime n (p * q)} := by
    72â†’  sorry
    73â†’
    74â†’end Erdos1110
    75â†’

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 872
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/872](https://www.erdosproblems.com/872)
    23â†’
    24â†’Consider the two-player game in which players alternately choose integers
    25â†’from $\{2, 3, \ldots, n\}$ to be included in some set $A$ such that no $a \mid b$ for
    26â†’$a \neq b \in A$ (i.e., $A$ remains a primitive set â€” an antichain under divisibility).
    27â†’The game ends when no legal move is possible.
    28â†’
    29â†’One player (the Lengthener) wants the game to last as long as possible;
    30â†’the other (the Shortener) wants it to end quickly.
    31â†’
    32â†’At least $\varepsilon n$ moves (for some $\varepsilon > 0$ and $n$ sufficiently large)?
    33â†’At least $(1 - \varepsilon) n / 2$ moves (for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and $n$ sufficiently large)?
    34â†’
    35â†’Erdإ‘s does not specify which player goes first, which may affect the answer.
    36â†’This is a type of saturation game.
    37â†’
    38â†’[Er92c] Erdإ‘s, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_,
    39â†’Matematiche (Catania) 47 (1992), 231â€“240.
    40â†’-/
    41â†’
    42â†’open Finset Classical
    43â†’
    44â†’namespace Erdos872
    45â†’
    46â†’/-- A finset of natural numbers is *primitive* (an antichain under divisibility)
    47â†’if $a \mid b$ with $a, b \in A$ implies $a = b$. -/
    48â†’def IsPrimitive (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    49â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, a âˆ£ b â†’ a = b
    50â†’
    51â†’/-- The universe of moves in the game: $\{2, 3, \ldots, n\}$. -/
    52â†’def gameUniverse (n : â„•) : Finset â„• :=
    53â†’  (Finset.range (n + 1)).filter (آ· â‰¥ 2)
    54â†’
    55â†’/-- Legal moves from a primitive position $A \subseteq \{2, \ldots, n\}$: elements $x$ in the
    56â†’universe not in $A$ such that inserting $x$ keeps the set primitive. -/
    57â†’noncomputable def legalMoves (n : â„•) (A : Finset â„•) : Finset â„• :=
    58â†’  (gameUniverse n \ A).filter fun x =>
    59â†’    âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, آ¬(a âˆ£ x) âˆ§ آ¬(x âˆ£ a)
    60â†’
    61â†’/-- A legal strategy for the primitive-set game on $\{2, \ldots, n\}$: given any
    62â†’game state with at least one legal move, it picks a legal move. -/
    63â†’def LegalStrategy (n : â„•) :=
    64â†’  (A : Finset â„•) â†’ (legalMoves n A).Nonempty â†’ {x // x âˆˆ legalMoves n A}
    65â†’
    66â†’/-- Play the game with given legal strategies for the Lengthener and Shortener.
    67â†’`lengthenerTurn = true` means it is the Lengthener's turn.
    68â†’The `fuel` parameter bounds recursion depth ($n + 1$ suffices).
    69â†’Returns the total number of moves played. -/
    70â†’noncomputable def playGame (n : â„•) (sL sS : LegalStrategy n) :
    71â†’    â„• â†’ Finset â„• â†’ Bool â†’ â„•
    72â†’  | 0, _, _ => 0
    73â†’  | fuel + 1, A, lengthenerTurn =>
    74â†’    if h : (legalMoves n A).Nonempty then
    75â†’      let strategy := if lengthenerTurn then sL else sS
    76â†’      let x := (strategy A h).val
    77â†’      1 + playGame n sL sS fuel (insert x A) (!lengthenerTurn)
    78â†’    else 0
    79â†’
    80â†’/--
    81â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 872 (weak form) [Er92c, p. 47]:
    82â†’
    83â†’In the primitive-set saturation game on $\{2, 3, \ldots, n\}$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$
    84â†’such that, regardless of who moves first, the Lengthener can guarantee
    85â†’at least $\varepsilon n$ moves for all sufficiently large $n$.
    86â†’-/
    87â†’@[category research open, AMS 5 91]
    88â†’theorem erdos_872 :
    89â†’    answer(sorry) â†”
    90â†’    âˆƒ خµ : â„‌, خµ > 0 âˆ§
    91â†’    âˆƒ Nâ‚€ : â„•, âˆ€ n â‰¥ Nâ‚€,
    92â†’      âˆ€ firstPlayer : Bool,
    93â†’        âˆƒ sL : LegalStrategy n, âˆ€ sS : LegalStrategy n,
    94â†’          (playGame n sL sS (n + 1) âˆ… firstPlayer : â„‌) â‰¥ خµ * (n : â„‌) := by
    95â†’  sorry
    96â†’
    97â†’/--
    98â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 872 (strong form) [Er92c, p. 47]:
    99â†’
   100â†’For every $\varepsilon > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$, regardless of who moves first,
   101â†’the Lengthener can guarantee at least $(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n / 2$ moves in the
   102â†’primitive-set saturation game on $\{2, 3, \ldots, n\}$.
   103â†’-/
   104â†’@[category research open, AMS 5 91]
   105â†’theorem erdos_872.variants.strong :
   106â†’    answer(sorry) â†”
   107â†’    âˆ€ خµ : â„‌, خµ > 0 â†’
   108â†’    âˆƒ Nâ‚€ : â„•, âˆ€ n â‰¥ Nâ‚€,
   109â†’      âˆ€ firstPlayer : Bool,
   110â†’        âˆƒ sL : LegalStrategy n, âˆ€ sS : LegalStrategy n,
   111â†’          (playGame n sL sS (n + 1) âˆ… firstPlayer : â„‌) â‰¥ (1 - خµ) * (n : â„‌) / 2 := by
   112â†’  sorry
   113â†’
   114â†’end Erdos872
   115â†’

    37â†’$a_{n+1} - a_n < n$ for all sufficiently large $n$?

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 877
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/877](https://www.erdosproblems.com/877)
    23â†’
    24â†’Let $f_m(n)$ count the number of maximal sum-free subsets $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ â€” that is,
    25â†’$A$ contains no three elements $a, b, c$ with $a = b + c$, and $A$ is maximal with this
    26â†’property (no element of $\{1, \ldots, n\} \setminus A$ can be added while preserving sum-freeness).
    27â†’
    28â†’Is it true that $f_m(n) = o(2^{n/2})$?
    29â†’
    30â†’Cameron and Erdإ‘s [CaEr90] proved that $f_m(n) > 2^{n/4}$. Luczak and Schoen [LuSc01] proved
    31â†’that there exists $c < 1/2$ with $f_m(n) < 2^{cn}$, resolving the conjecture.
    32â†’Balogh, Liu, Sharifzadeh, and Treglown [BLST15] proved $f_m(n) = 2^{(1/4+o(1))n}$,
    33â†’later refined [BLST18] to $f_m(n) = (C_n + o(1)) \cdot 2^{n/4}$.
    34â†’-/
    35â†’
    36â†’open scoped Classical
    37â†’open Finset
    38â†’
    39â†’namespace Erdos877
    40â†’
    41â†’/-- A finite set of natural numbers is *sum-free* if for all $b, c \in A$,
    42â†’we have $b + c \notin A$. -/
    43â†’def IsSumFree (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    44â†’  âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, b + c âˆ‰ A
    45â†’
    46â†’/-- A subset $A$ of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$ is a *maximal sum-free subset* if it is sum-free
    47â†’and no element of $\{1, \ldots, n\} \setminus A$ can be added while preserving
    48â†’sum-freeness. -/
    49â†’def IsMaximalSumFree (A : Finset â„•) (n : â„•) : Prop :=
    50â†’  A âٹ† Finset.Icc 1 n âˆ§
    51â†’  IsSumFree A âˆ§
    52â†’  âˆ€ x âˆˆ Finset.Icc 1 n, x âˆ‰ A â†’ آ¬ IsSumFree (insert x A)
    53â†’
    54â†’/-- The number of maximal sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. -/
    55â†’noncomputable def maximalSumFreeCount (n : â„•) : â„• :=
    56â†’  ((Finset.Icc 1 n).powerset.filter (fun A => IsMaximalSumFree A n)).card
    57â†’
    58â†’/--
    59â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 877 [CaEr90][Er98]:
    60â†’
    61â†’Let $f_m(n)$ count the number of maximal sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. Then
    62â†’$$f_m(n) = o(2^{n/2}).$$
    63â†’
    64â†’That is, for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$,
    65â†’$$f_m(n) \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^{n/2}.$$
    66â†’
    67â†’Proved by Luczak and Schoen [LuSc01], with the sharp asymptotics
    68â†’$f_m(n) = 2^{(1/4+o(1))n}$ established by Baloghâ€“Liuâ€“Sharifzadehâ€“Treglown [BLST15].
    69â†’-/
    70â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    71â†’theorem erdos_877 :
    72â†’    answer(True) â†”
    73â†’    âˆ€ خµ : â„‌, خµ > 0 â†’
    74â†’    âˆƒ Nâ‚€ : â„•, âˆ€ n : â„•, n â‰¥ Nâ‚€ â†’
    75â†’      (maximalSumFreeCount n : â„‌) â‰¤ خµ * (2 : â„‌) ^ ((n : â„‌) / 2) := by
    76â†’  sorry
    77â†’
    78â†’end Erdos877
    79â†’

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 748
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/748](https://www.erdosproblems.com/748)
    23â†’
    24â†’The Cameron-Erdإ‘s Conjecture. Let $f(n)$ count the number of sum-free subsets
    25â†’$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$, i.e., $A$ contains no three elements $a$, $b$, $c$ with
    26â†’$a = b + c$. Is it true that $f(n) = 2^{(1+o(1)) n/2}$?
    27â†’
    28â†’The lower bound $f(n) \geq 2^{n/2}$ is trivial (consider all subsets of
    29â†’$\{\lceil n/2 \rceil, \ldots, n\}$).
    30â†’
    31â†’This was proved independently by Green [Gr04] and Sapozhenko [Sa03], who showed
    32â†’$f(n) \sim c_n \cdot 2^{n/2}$ where $c_n$ depends on the parity of $n$.
    33â†’
    34â†’[CaEr90] Cameron, P.J. and Erdإ‘s, P.
    35â†’
    36â†’[Er94b] Erdإ‘s, P.
    37â†’
    38â†’[Er98] Erdإ‘s, P.
    39â†’
    40â†’[Gr04] Green, B., _The Cameron-Erdإ‘s conjecture_, Bull. London Math. Soc. 36 (2004), 769-778.
    41â†’
    42â†’[Sa03] Sapozhenko, A.A., _The Cameron-Erdإ‘s conjecture_, Discrete Math. 235 (2003), 131-138.
    43â†’-/
    44â†’
    45â†’open Finset
    46â†’
    47â†’namespace Erdos748
    48â†’
    49â†’/-- A finite set of natural numbers is *sum-free* if it contains no three
    50â†’    elements $a$, $b$, $c$ (not necessarily distinct) with $b + c = a$. Equivalently,
    51â†’    for all $b, c \in A$, we have $b + c \notin A$. -/
    52â†’def IsSumFree (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    53â†’  âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, b + c âˆ‰ A
    54â†’
    55â†’instance : DecidablePred IsSumFree :=
    56â†’  fun A => decidable_of_iff (âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, b + c âˆ‰ A) Iff.rfl
    57â†’
    58â†’/-- The number of sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. -/
    59â†’noncomputable def sumFreeSubsetCount (n : â„•) : â„• :=
    60â†’  ((Finset.Icc 1 n).powerset.filter (fun A => IsSumFree A)).card
    61â†’
    62â†’/--
    63â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 748 (Cameron-Erdإ‘s Conjecture) [CaEr90][Er94b][Er98]:
    64â†’
    65â†’Let $f(n)$ count the number of sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. Then
    66â†’$$f(n) = 2^{(1+o(1)) n/2}.$$
    67â†’
    68â†’Equivalently: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$,
    69â†’$$2^{(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n/2} \leq f(n) \leq 2^{(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n/2}.$$
    70â†’
    71â†’Proved independently by Green [Gr04] and Sapozhenko [Sa03].
    72â†’-/
    73â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    74â†’theorem erdos_748 : answer(True) â†”
    75â†’    âˆ€ خµ : â„‌, خµ > 0 â†’
    76â†’    âˆƒ Nâ‚€ : â„•, âˆ€ n : â„•, n â‰¥ Nâ‚€ â†’
    77â†’      (2 : â„‌) ^ ((1 - خµ) * (n : â„‌) / 2) â‰¤ (sumFreeSubsetCount n : â„‌) âˆ§
    78â†’      (sumFreeSubsetCount n : â„‌) â‰¤ (2 : â„‌) ^ ((1 + خµ) * (n : â„‌) / 2) := by
    79â†’  sorry
    80â†’
    81â†’end Erdos748
    82â†’

    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 316
    21â†’
    22â†’*References:*
    23â†’- [erdosproblems.com/316](https://www.erdosproblems.com/316)
    24â†’- [Sa97] Sأ،ndor, Csaba, On a problem of Erdإ‘s. J. Number Theory (1997), 203-210.
    25â†’-/
    26â†’
    27â†’namespace Erdos316
    28â†’
    29â†’/--
    30â†’Is it true that if $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}\setminus\{1\}$ is a finite set with
    31â†’$\sum_{n \in A} \frac{1}{n} < 2$ then there is a partition $A=A_1 \sqcup A_2$
    32â†’such that $\sum_{n \in A_i} \frac{1}{n} < 1$ for $i=1,2$?
    33â†’
    34â†’This is not true in general, as shown by Sأ،ndor [Sa97].
    35â†’
    36â†’The minimal counterexample is $\{2,3,4,5,6,7,10,11,13,14,15\}$, found by Tom Stobart.
    37â†’
    38â†’This was formalized in Lean by Mehta.
    39â†’-/
    40â†’@[category research formally solved using lean4 at
    41â†’"https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures/blob/main/FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/316.lean",
    42â†’AMS 5 11]
    43â†’theorem erdos_316 : answer(False) â†” âˆ€ A : Finset â„•, 0 âˆ‰ A â†’ 1 âˆ‰ A â†’
    44â†’    âˆ‘ n âˆˆ A, (1 / n : â„ڑ) < 2 â†’ âˆƒ (Aâ‚پ Aâ‚‚ : Finset â„•),
    45â†’      Disjoint Aâ‚پ Aâ‚‚ âˆ§ A = Aâ‚پ âˆھ Aâ‚‚ âˆ§
    46â†’      âˆ‘ n âˆˆ Aâ‚پ, (1 / n : â„ڑ) < 1 âˆ§ âˆ‘ n âˆˆ Aâ‚‚, (1 / n : â„ڑ) <  1 := by
    47â†’  simp only [one_div, false_iff, not_forall, not_exists, not_and, not_lt]
    48â†’  let A : Finset â„• := {2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15}
    49â†’  refine âں¨A, by decide, by decide, by decide +kernel, ?_âں©
    50â†’  suffices h : âˆ€ B âٹ† A, âˆ‘ n âˆˆ B, (n : â„ڑ)âپ»آ¹ < 1 â†’ 1 â‰¤ âˆ‘ n âˆˆ A \ B, (n : â„ڑ)âپ»آ¹ by
    51â†’    rintro B C hBC hA hlt
    52â†’    have : C = A \ B := by rw [hA, Finset.union_sdiff_cancel_left hBC]
    53â†’    exact this â–¸ h B (by simp [hA]) hlt
    54â†’  decide +kernel
    55â†’
    56â†’/-- This is not true if $A$ is a multiset, for example $2,3,3,5,5,5,5$. -/
    57â†’@[category high_school, AMS 5 11]
    58â†’lemma erdos_316.variants.multiset : âˆƒ A : Multiset â„•, 0 âˆ‰ A âˆ§ 1 âˆ‰ A âˆ§
    59â†’    (A.map ((1 : â„ڑ) / آ·)).sum < 2 âˆ§ âˆ€ (Aâ‚پ Aâ‚‚ : Multiset â„•),
    60â†’      A = Aâ‚پ + Aâ‚‚ â†’
    61â†’        1 â‰¤ (Aâ‚پ.map ((1 : â„ڑ) / آ·)).sum âˆ¨ 1 â‰¤ (Aâ‚‚.map ((1 : â„ڑ) / آ·)).sum := by
    62â†’  let A : Multiset â„• := {2, 3, 3, 5, 5, 5, 5}
    63â†’  refine âں¨A, by decide, by decide, by decide +kernel, ?_âں©
    64â†’  suffices h : âˆ€ B âˆˆ A.powerset, 1 â‰¤ (B.map (fun x â†¦ (x : â„ڑ)âپ»آ¹)).sum âˆ¨
    65â†’      1 â‰¤ ((A - B).map (fun x â†¦ (x : â„ڑ)âپ»آ¹)).sum by
    66â†’    intro B C hBC
    67â†’    have : C = A - B := by simp [hBC]
    68â†’    simp only [Multiset.pure_def, Multiset.bind_def, Multiset.bind_singleton, Multiset.map_map,
    69â†’      Function.comp_apply, one_div] at h âٹ¢
    70â†’    exact this â–¸ h B (by simp [hBC])
    71â†’  decide +kernel
    72â†’
    73â†’/--
    74â†’This is not true in general, as shown by Sأ،ndor [Sa97], who observed that the proper divisors of
    75â†’$120$ form a counterexample. More generally, Sأ،ndor shows that for any $n\geq 2$ there exists a
    76â†’finite set $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}\backslash\{1\}$ with $\sum_{k\in A}\frac{1}{k} < n$ and no
    77â†’partition into $n$ parts each of which has $\sum_{k\in A_i}\frac{1}{k}<1$.
    78â†’-/
    79â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    80â†’theorem erdos_316.variants.generalized (n : â„•) (hn : 2 â‰¤ n) : âˆƒ A : Finset â„•,
    81â†’    A.Nonempty âˆ§ 0 âˆ‰ A âˆ§ 1 âˆ‰ A âˆ§ âˆ‘ k âˆˆ A, (1 / k : â„ڑ) < n âˆ§ âˆ€ P : Finpartition A,
    82â†’    P.parts.card = n â†’ âˆƒ p âˆˆ P.parts, 1 â‰¤ âˆ‘ n âˆˆ p, (1 / n : â„ڑ) := by
    83â†’  sorry
    84â†’
    85â†’end Erdos316
    86â†’

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 497
    21â†’
    22â†’How many antichains are there in the power set of an n-element set?
    23â†’
    24â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/497](https://www.erdosproblems.com/497)
    25â†’
    26â†’[Kl69] Kleitman, D., *On Dedekind's Problem: The Number of Monotone Boolean Functions*.
    27â†’Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. 21 (1969), 677-682.
    28â†’-/
    29â†’
    30â†’open Finset Filter
    31â†’
    32â†’namespace Erdos497
    33â†’
    34â†’open Classical in
    35â†’/--
    36â†’The number of antichains in the power set of $\operatorname{Fin} n$ (the Dedekind number $D(n)$).
    37â†’An antichain is a family $F$ of subsets of $[n]$ such that for all $A, B \in F$,
    38â†’$A \neq B$ implies $A \not\subset B$.
    39â†’-/
    40â†’noncomputable def dedekindNumber (n : â„•) : â„• :=
    41â†’  Fintype.card {F : Finset (Finset (Fin n)) // IsAntichain (آ· âٹ† آ·) (F : Set (Finset (Fin n)))}
    42â†’
    43â†’/--
    44â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 497 (Dedekind's Problem, resolved by Kleitman [Kl69]):
    45â†’How many antichains in $[n]$ are there? That is, how many families of subsets
    46â†’of $[n]$ are there such that no member is a subset of another?
    47â†’
    48â†’Sperner's theorem states that any single antichain has size at most
    49â†’$\binom{n}{\lfloor n/2 \rfloor}$. Kleitman proved that the total number of antichains in the
    50â†’power set of $[n]$ is $2^{(1+o(1)) \binom{n}{\lfloor n/2 \rfloor}}$.
    51â†’
    52â†’Equivalently, $\log_2(D(n)) / \binom{n}{\lfloor n/2 \rfloor} \to 1$ as $n \to \infty$.
    53â†’-/
    54â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5 6]
    55â†’theorem erdos_497 :
    56â†’    Tendsto
    57â†’      (fun n : â„• => Real.log (dedekindNumber n : â„‌) / (Real.log 2 * (n.choose (n / 2) : â„‌)))
    58â†’      atTop (nhds 1) := by
    59â†’  sorry
    60â†’
    61â†’end Erdos497
    62â†’

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 776
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/776](https://www.erdosproblems.com/776)
    23â†’
    24â†’A problem of Erdإ‘s and Trotter. Let $r \geq 2$ and $A_1, \ldots, A_m \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$
    25â†’be an antichain such that every set size that appears does so at least $r$ times. How large must
    26â†’$n$ be (as a function of $r$) to ensure such a family achieves $n - 3$ distinct set sizes?
    27â†’
    28â†’For $r = 1$ and $n > 3$ the maximum possible number of distinct sizes is $n - 2$. For $r > 1$ and
    29â†’$n$ sufficiently large, $n - 3$ is achievable but $n - 2$ is never achievable.
    30â†’-/
    31â†’
    32â†’namespace Erdos776
    33â†’
    34â†’/-- A family of sets is an antichain if no set is a subset of another. -/
    35â†’def IsAntichain (F : Finset (Finset â„•)) : Prop :=
    36â†’  âˆ€ A âˆˆ F, âˆ€ B âˆˆ F, A â‰  B â†’ آ¬(A âٹ† B)
    37â†’
    38â†’/-- Every size that appears in the family appears at least $r$ times. -/
    39â†’def HasMinMultiplicity (F : Finset (Finset â„•)) (r : â„•) : Prop :=
    40â†’  âˆ€ t : â„•, (âˆƒ A âˆˆ F, A.card = t) â†’
    41â†’    r â‰¤ (F.filter (fun A => A.card = t)).card
    42â†’
    43â†’/-- The number of distinct sizes of sets in the family. -/
    44â†’def numDistinctSizes (F : Finset (Finset â„•)) : â„• :=
    45â†’  (F.image Finset.card).card
    46â†’
    47â†’/-- All sets in the family are subsets of $\{0, \ldots, n-1\}$. -/
    48â†’def AllSubsetsOfRange (F : Finset (Finset â„•)) (n : â„•) : Prop :=
    49â†’  âˆ€ A âˆˆ F, âˆ€ x âˆˆ A, x < n
    50â†’
    51â†’/--
    52â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 776:
    53â†’For every $r \geq 2$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$ there is an antichain
    54â†’$F$ of subsets of $\{0, \ldots, n-1\}$ in which every size class has at least $r$ members
    55â†’and the family achieves exactly $n - 3$ distinct sizes of sets.
    56â†’
    57â†’The problem asks for the growth rate of $N_0$ as a function of $r$.
    58â†’-/
    59â†’@[category research open, AMS 5]
    60â†’theorem erdos_776 :
    61â†’    âˆ€ r : â„•, r â‰¥ 2 â†’
    62â†’      âˆƒ Nâ‚€ : â„•, âˆ€ n : â„•, n â‰¥ Nâ‚€ â†’
    63â†’        âˆƒ F : Finset (Finset â„•),
    64â†’          AllSubsetsOfRange F n âˆ§
    65â†’          IsAntichain F âˆ§
    66â†’          HasMinMultiplicity F r âˆ§
    67â†’          numDistinctSizes F = n - 3 := by
    68â†’  sorry
    69â†’
    70â†’/--
    71â†’For $r = 1$ and $n > 3$, an antichain of subsets of $\{0, \ldots, n-1\}$ can achieve
    72â†’at most $n - 2$ distinct sizes, and this is tight.
    73â†’-/
    74â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    75â†’theorem erdos_776.variants.r_eq_1 :
    76â†’    âˆ€ n : â„•, n > 3 â†’
    77â†’      (âˆƒ F : Finset (Finset â„•),
    78â†’        AllSubsetsOfRange F n âˆ§
    79â†’        IsAntichain F âˆ§
    80â†’        numDistinctSizes F = n - 2) âˆ§
    81â†’      (âˆ€ F : Finset (Finset â„•),
    82â†’        AllSubsetsOfRange F n â†’
    83â†’        IsAntichain F â†’
    84â†’        numDistinctSizes F â‰¤ n - 2) := by
    85â†’  sorry
    86â†’
    87â†’/--
    88â†’For $r \geq 2$, no antichain with minimum multiplicity $r$ can achieve $n - 2$
    89â†’distinct sizes (for any $n$).
    90â†’-/

   142â†’/-- Only the empty set is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/

   144â†’

   149â†’equal. -/

   151â†’    (hâ‚پ : s.IsAPOfLength lâ‚پ) (hâ‚‚ : s.IsAPOfLength lâ‚‚) :

   166â†’
   167â†’/-- Only the empty list is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/
   168â†’@[simp] theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLength 0 â†” s = [] := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   169â†’
   170â†’/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/

   172â†’

   181â†’
   182â†’theorem Set.isAPOfLength_pair {خ± : Type*} [DecidableEq خ±] [AddCommGroup خ±] {a b : خ±} (hab : a â‰  b) :

   194â†’
   195â†’/-- We say that a set `s` is free of arithmetic progressions of length `l` if `s` contains no

   102â†’/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A âٹ† {1, â€¦, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103â†’`{1, â€¦, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/
   104â†’def IsMaximalSidonSetIn (A : Set â„•) (N : â„•) : Prop :=
   105â†’  A âٹ† Set.Icc 1 N âˆ§ IsSidon A âˆ§
   106â†’    âˆ€ â¦ƒx : â„•â¦„, x âˆˆ Set.Icc 1 N â†’ x âˆ‰ A â†’ آ¬ IsSidon (A âˆھ {x})
   107â†’
   108â†’namespace IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   109â†’
   110â†’/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, â€¦, N}`, then `A âٹ† {1, â€¦, N}`. -/
   111â†’theorem subset {A : Set â„•} {N : â„•} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) :
   112â†’    A âٹ† Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1
   113â†’
   114â†’/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, â€¦, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/
   115â†’theorem isSidon {A : Set â„•} {N : â„•} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) : IsSidon A := hA.2.1
   116â†’
   117â†’/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/
   118â†’theorem maximal {A : Set â„•} {N : â„•} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) {x : â„•}
   119â†’    (hx : x âˆˆ Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x âˆ‰ A) : آ¬ IsSidon (A âˆھ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA
   120â†’
   121â†’end IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   122â†’
   123â†’end Set
   124â†’
   125â†’namespace Finset
   126â†’
   127â†’instance (A : Finset خ±) [DecidableEq خ±] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set خ±)) :=
   128â†’  decidable_of_iff (âˆ€لµ‰ (iâ‚پ âˆˆ A) (jâ‚پ âˆˆ A) (iâ‚‚ âˆˆ A) (jâ‚‚ âˆˆ A), _) <| by rfl
   129â†’
   130â†’/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/
   131â†’def maxSidonSubsetCard (A : Finset خ±) [DecidableEq خ±] : â„• :=
   132â†’  (A.powerset.filter fun B : Finset خ± â†¦ IsSidon (B : Set خ±)).sup Finset.card
   133â†’
   134â†’/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A âˆھ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s â‰¥ A.max + 1`. -/
   135â†’theorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) {s : â„•}
   136â†’    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 â‰¤ s) :
   137â†’    IsSidon (A âˆھ {s}) := by
   138â†’  have hâ‚پ {a b c : â„•} (ha : a âˆˆ A) (hb : b âˆˆ A) (hc : c âˆˆ A) :
   139â†’        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140â†’  have : s âˆ‰ A := by
   141â†’    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   142â†’      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143â†’  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb â†¦
   144â†’    âں¨by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc â†¦ by linarith [hâ‚پ hc hb ha]âں©
   145â†’
   146â†’theorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) :
   147â†’    âˆƒ m âˆ‰ A, IsSidon (A âˆھ {m}) := by
   148â†’  refine âں¨2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rflâں©
   149â†’  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   150â†’    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151â†’
   152â†’theorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) (s : â„•) :
   153â†’    âˆƒ m â‰¥ s, m âˆ‰ A âˆ§ IsSidon (A âˆھ {m}) := by
   154â†’  refine âں¨if s â‰¥ 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_âں©
   155â†’  آ· split_ifs <;> linarith
   156â†’  آ· split_ifs <;>
   157â†’    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   158â†’      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159â†’  آ· split_ifs with hs
   160â†’    آ· exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161â†’    آ· exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl
   162â†’

    25â†’if every member `n` of a sequence has the property that `m * n` is also in the sequence
    26â†’for coprime `m`, then the primitive elements generate the entire sequence.
